sourceData.description: NebulaPulsar is a proof-of-concept in-memory implant framework for Java (JSP) and ASP.NET (ASPX/ASHX/ASMX) webshells, originally developed as part of the Alien project.

bodyHtml:
NebulaPulsar is making waves in the Backend ecosystem this week, gaining 0new stars. With a total of 35 stars on GitHub and 7 forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 35Stars
📈 +0/wkGrowth

Key Features

  • Written in Java for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://iss4cf0ng.github.io/2026/06/27/2026-6-27-NebulaPulsar/
  • 🏷️ csharp
  • 🏷️ fileless
  • 🏷️ im-memory
  • 🏷️ implant
  • 🏷️ java

Why NebulaPulsar Is Trending

The Backend landscape is constantly evolving, and NebulaPulsar has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 0 new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: NebulaPulsar is a proof-of-concept in-memory implant framework for Java (JSP) and ASP.NET (ASPX/ASHX/ASMX) webshells, originally developed as part of the Alien project.

If you're working in Backend, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Quick Start

Get started with NebulaPulsar in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/iss4cf0ng/NebulaPulsar # Navigate to project cd NebulaPulsar # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

NebulaPulsar is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
